response.justification: Bogdan has a long and varied software career with genuine strengths in developer tooling and software craftsmanship, but his experience is almost entirely in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Java appears only briefly in his skills list and in a couple of minor role mentions, and there is no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and no JVM-depth work anywhere in the resume. His one IDE-adjacent role (NI LabWindows/CVI) was C/C++-based, not JVM. The core technical requirements of this role — deep JVM expertise, enterprise Java/Kotlin frameworks, and IDE internals — are not demonstrated.
strengths: Genuine tooling and developer-experience background: built MCP code generators, test runners, and assertion libraries — shows affinity for the meta-layer of software
Strong software craftsmanship discipline: consistent TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development across multiple roles
Experience contributing to a C-based IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) and organizing internal code-quality communities, indicating interest in developer tooling culture
concerns: No Kotlin experience anywhere in the resume — a hard requirement for this role
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience mentioned across 15+ years of work history
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java is listed in skills but never featured as a core language in any role
